8629	\begin{lemma}
8630	[An additive functor commutes with augmentation, degreewise the identity]
8631	  \label{lem:mapHC_augment_iso}
8632	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.mapHC_augment_iso}
8633	  Let \(\Phi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be an additive functor between preadditive
8634	  categories, \(C^\bullet\) a cochain complex in \(\mathcal{A}\), and \(f : Y \to C^0\) an
8635	  augmentation map satisfying \(f \cdot d^0 = 0\). Then applying \(\Phi\) degreewise commutes
8636	  with the augmentation construction: there is an isomorphism of cochain complexes
8637	  \[
8638	    \Phi\bigl(C^\bullet.\operatorname{augment} f\bigr)
8639	      \;\cong\;
8640	    \bigl(\Phi C^\bullet\bigr).\operatorname{augment}\bigl(\Phi f\bigr),
8641	  \]
8642	  every component of which is the identity. (Here \(\Phi f\) satisfies its own augmentation
8643	  identity \(\Phi(f) \cdot d^0 = 0\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:map_augment_cond}.)
8644	\end{lemma}
8645	\begin{proof}
8646	  \uses{lem:map_augment_cond}
8647	  The two augmented complexes have, by construction, the same underlying graded object:
8648	  in degree \(0\) both read \(\Phi Y\), and in degree \(n+1\) both read \(\Phi C^n\). Build
8649	  the isomorphism with \(\operatorname{isoOfComponents}\), taking every component to be the
8650	  identity \(\mathrm{id}\). The differential-compatibility square in degree \(0\) and in each
8651	  degree \(n+1\) then closes by unfolding the definition of \(\operatorname{augment}\): the
8652	  augmented differentials of \(\Phi(C^\bullet.\operatorname{augment} f)\) and of
8653	  \((\Phi C^\bullet).\operatorname{augment}(\Phi f)\) are literally the same map
8654	  (\(\Phi f\) in the bottom slot, \(\Phi(d^{n})\) above), so each square commutes on the nose.
8655	\end{proof}
8656	
8657	\begin{lemma}
8658	[Additive functors preserve the augmentation condition]
8659	  \label{lem:map_augment_cond}
8660	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.map_augment_cond}
8661	  Let \(\Phi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be an additive functor between preadditive
8662	  categories, \(C^\bullet\) a cochain complex in \(\mathcal{A}\), and \(f : Y \to C^0\) a map
8663	  with \(f \cdot d^0 = 0\). Then the image map \(\Phi f : \Phi Y \to \Phi C^0\) again satisfies
8664	  the augmentation condition \(\Phi(f) \cdot d^0 = 0\), where \(d^0\) is the lowest differential
8665	  of the degreewise image complex \(\Phi C^\bullet\).
8666	\end{lemma}
8667	\begin{proof}
8668	  The lowest differential of \(\Phi C^\bullet\) is, by definitional reshaping, \(\Phi(d^0)\)
8669	  applied to the original lowest differential of \(C^\bullet\). Hence
8670	  \(\Phi(f) \cdot \Phi(d^0) = \Phi(f \cdot d^0) = \Phi(0) = 0\) by functoriality of \(\Phi\)
8671	  on composites and \(\Phi.\!\operatorname{map\_zero}\).
8672	\end{proof}

8674	\begin{lemma}
8675	[Gluing an iso of augmented complexes from base, node, and compatibility data]
8676	  \label{lem:augmentCochainIso}
8677	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.augmentCochainIso}
8678	  Let \(C^\bullet, C'^\bullet\) be cochain complexes in a preadditive category, with augmentation
8679	  maps \(f : Y \to C^0\) (\(f \cdot d^0 = 0\)) and \(f' : Y' \to C'^0\) (\(f' \cdot d'^0 = 0\)).
8680	  Given an isomorphism \(\varphi : C^\bullet \cong C'^\bullet\) of the base complexes, an
8681	  isomorphism \(e_Y : Y \cong Y'\) of the augmentation objects, and a compatibility square
8682	  \(f \cdot \varphi_0 = e_Y \cdot f'\) intertwining the augmentation maps through \(e_Y\) and the
8683	  degree-\(0\) component \(\varphi_0\) of \(\varphi\), there is an isomorphism of augmented
8684	  cochain complexes
8685	  \[
8686	    C^\bullet.\operatorname{augment} f \;\cong\; C'^\bullet.\operatorname{augment} f'.
8687	  \]
8688	\end{lemma}
8689	\begin{proof}
8690	  Build the isomorphism with \(\operatorname{isoOfComponents}\): take the degree-\(0\) component
8691	  to be \(e_Y\) and the degree-\(n+1\) component to be \(\varphi_n\), the \(n\)-th component of
8692	  \(\varphi\). The degree-\(0\) differential square is exactly the supplied compatibility square
8693	  \(f \cdot \varphi_0 = e_Y \cdot f'\). For each degree \(n+1\), the augmented differential is the
8694	  original differential of the base complex (the augmentation only modifies the bottom slot), so
8695	  the square reduces to the naturality square \(\varphi.\!\operatorname{comm}(n, n+1)\) of
8696	  \(\varphi\). Hence all squares commute and the components assemble to an isomorphism.
8697	\end{proof}
8698

8700	[Open-meet identity: intersection with \(V\) distributes over the cover meet]
8701	  \label{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}

8703	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
8704	  Let \(\mathcal{U}\) be an open cover of \(X\), let \(V \subseteq X\) be an open, and let
8705	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I\) be a multi-index. Then, as opens of \(X\),
8706	  \[
8707	    \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V
8708	      \;=\;
8709	    \bigcap_{k}\bigl(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k) \cap V\bigr).
8710	  \]
8711	\end{lemma}
8712	\begin{proof}
8713	  By definition \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma = \bigcap_k
8714	  \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\), the \((p+1)\)-fold meet of the
8715	  cover-member opens. The index type \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\) is nonempty, and in the frame of
8716	  opens a binary meet distributes over a \emph{nonempty} indexed meet:
8717	  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k A_k\bigr) \cap V = \bigcap_k (A_k \cap V)\). (Nonemptiness is essential:
8718	  over the empty index the left side is \(\top \cap V = V\) while the right side is \(\top\).)
